meta.title: The Sourcing Screen
meta.subtitle: AI-enabled service roll-ups · five-box thesis screen · illustrative pipeline
meta.disclaimer: Every company, financial figure, and deal note below is fictional — built to demonstrate a sourcing/screening framework, not to represent real targets, real data, or any employer's actual pipeline or thesis.

thesis.headline: Where PE actually fits in the AI stack isn't the labs — it's the fragmented, already-profitable service businesses that can use AI to expand margin on a cash-flow base that already exists.
thesis.body: Frontier labs, compute, and most AI-native applications are still venture and strategic-capital territory: pre-cash-flow, capital-intensive, or racing for share at the expense of margin. The roll-up opportunity sits one layer down — in ordinary SMB service businesses (bookkeeping, IT support, medical billing, home services) where AI isn't the product, it's a lever on an existing P&L. This screen sources and ranks targets against that specific thesis.

criteria.label: Market Fragmentation
criteria.description: How many similar operators exist in the target's geography and niche, and how much of the market is still unconsolidated — the raw runway for a roll-up.

criteria.label: Unit Economics
criteria.description: EBITDA margin quality, cash conversion, and revenue durability — the underwriting case independent of any AI story.

criteria.label: AI-Adoption Leverage
criteria.description: How much margin AI can realistically unlock in this specific workflow — the thesis-specific multiplier, not a generic "add AI" hand-wave.

criteria.label: Moat & Stickiness
criteria.description: Contract structure, switching costs, and data lock-in that protect the business — and the roll-up — once AI raises the bar for competitors too.

criteria.label: Exit Path
criteria.description: Whether this is a platform asset that re-rates the whole roll-up, or an accretive bolt-on — and how comparable multiples are trending.

deals.name: Cascade IT Partners

rationale.fragmentation: Regional MSP market with 40+ sub-scale competitors under $10M revenue — wide consolidation runway.
rationale.unitEconomics: 21% EBITDA margin with 92% recurring-contract revenue.
rationale.aiLeverage: Tier-1 ticket triage and patch-management scripting are largely automatable — material headcount leverage.
rationale.moat: Multi-year MSAs but low switching costs once a contract lapses.
rationale.exitPath: Strong platform candidate — anchor asset for a regional MSP roll-up.

deals.thesisNote: Lead candidate for the platform slot; diligence focused on customer concentration (top 5 = 38% of revenue).

deals.name: Northgate Networks

rationale.fragmentation: Same fragmented metro market as most sub-$5M MSPs.
rationale.unitEconomics: Margin thin for the vertical — largely project (non-recurring) revenue mix.
rationale.aiLeverage: Would benefit from automation, but the owner has resisted tooling changes historically.
rationale.moat: Month-to-month contracts, no lock-in.
rationale.exitPath: Bolt-on at best; not platform-grade on its own.

deals.thesisNote: Only proceed as a bolt-on under an existing platform, not standalone.

deals.name: Anchor Point IT

rationale.fragmentation: Fragmented market, but too small to move the needle alone.
rationale.unitEconomics: Sub-8% margin and one customer at 61% of revenue.
rationale.aiLeverage: Some ticket-automation upside, but not enough to fix the underlying margin problem.
rationale.moat: No contracts — pure handshake relationships.
rationale.exitPath: No credible path to platform or attractive bolt-on economics.

deals.thesisNote: Passed — customer concentration and margin both fail the screen independent of AI story.

deals.name: Ridgeline Bookkeeping Partners

rationale.fragmentation: Bookkeeping is one of the most fragmented SMB service categories in the country.
rationale.unitEconomics: 27% margin, 96% recurring monthly retainers, near-zero bad debt.
rationale.aiLeverage: Reconciliation and month-end close are highly automatable — the clearest AI-leverage case in the pipeline.
rationale.moat: Retainer relationships are sticky in practice, though contractually thin.
rationale.exitPath: Best platform candidate sourced to date — category leader multiple on exit.

deals.thesisNote: Top-ranked deal in the pipeline. LOI signed; targeting close in Q4.

deals.name: Harborview Accounting Group

rationale.fragmentation: Dense regional market, similar dynamics to Ridgeline.
rationale.unitEconomics: Healthy margin, slightly smaller scale.
rationale.aiLeverage: Same close/reconciliation automation upside as the vertical broadly.
rationale.moat: Long-tenured client base, informal but sticky.
rationale.exitPath: Solid bolt-on under a bookkeeping platform.

deals.thesisNote: Natural bolt-on once Ridgeline closes — same vertical, adjacent geography.

deals.name: Fieldstone CPA Services

rationale.fragmentation: Large fragmented Midwest market.
rationale.unitEconomics: Strong margin at meaningful scale.
rationale.aiLeverage: Heavier tax-compliance mix than pure bookkeeping — less automatable than reconciliation work.
rationale.moat: Engagement letters renew annually with high retention.
rationale.exitPath: Credible second platform if Ridgeline's geography doesn't extend this far.

deals.thesisNote: IOI sent; evaluating as a second regional platform vs. bolt-on.

deals.name: Lumen Creative Group

rationale.fragmentation: Agencies are fragmented, but differentiation is mostly reputational, not structural.
rationale.unitEconomics: Mid-teens margin, typical for the category.
rationale.aiLeverage: Generative tooling cuts production time, but also compresses the billable-hour model that funds the margin.
rationale.moat: Relationship-driven, minimal contractual lock-in.
rationale.exitPath: Key-person risk caps multiple; agency roll-ups have a mixed track record.

deals.thesisNote: Watching, not pursuing — AI is double-edged here, not a clean lever.

deals.name: Brightline Digital

rationale.fragmentation: Same fragmented agency landscape.
rationale.unitEconomics: Sub-7% margin, founder-dependent pricing.
rationale.aiLeverage: AI is more threat than lever — clients increasingly do this in-house with the same tools.
rationale.moat: No contracts, monthly churn.
rationale.exitPath: No platform or bolt-on case at this size and margin.

deals.thesisNote: Passed — textbook example of AI eroding the exact service being sold.

deals.name: Foundry Brand Partners

rationale.fragmentation: Fragmented, mid-size regional player.
rationale.unitEconomics: Above-average margin for an agency.
rationale.aiLeverage: Some production-cost savings, unproven at scale.
rationale.moat: A few multi-year retainer clients provide partial stability.
rationale.exitPath: Only a bolt-on candidate, and a marginal one.

deals.thesisNote: Low priority — sourced opportunistically, not pursuing actively.

deals.name: Summit Home Services

rationale.fragmentation: HVAC/plumbing is the archetypal fragmented roll-up category — thousands of owner-operators nationally.
rationale.unitEconomics: 20% margin with strong recurring maintenance-contract revenue.
rationale.aiLeverage: Dispatch routing and scheduling automation help, but the core service is still a truck and a technician.
rationale.moat: Long-tenured maintenance contracts and strong local brand density.
rationale.exitPath: Textbook home-services platform — well-precedented roll-up category with reliable multiple expansion.

deals.thesisNote: Second platform slot; diligence nearly complete, financing being finalized.

deals.name: GreenAcre Landscaping

rationale.fragmentation: Highly fragmented, seasonal category.
rationale.unitEconomics: Margin depressed by seasonality and equipment capex.
rationale.aiLeverage: Route optimization is the main lever — moderate, not transformative.
rationale.moat: Recurring seasonal contracts provide some stickiness.
rationale.exitPath: Reasonable bolt-on under Summit's platform if geographies align.

deals.thesisNote: Evaluating as a bolt-on to Summit rather than standalone.

deals.name: BluePeak Plumbing & Air

rationale.fragmentation: Fragmented West Coast metro market.
rationale.unitEconomics: Consistent 20% margin over three years of financials.
rationale.aiLeverage: AI-assisted dispatch and inventory forecasting rolled out post-close.
rationale.moat: Membership-style maintenance plans drive repeat revenue.
rationale.exitPath: First platform closed in the vertical — now the integration template for future bolt-ons.

deals.thesisNote: Closed. Now the reference case for underwriting future home-services deals.

deals.name: Vantage Staffing Solutions

rationale.fragmentation: Fragmented, but large national players compete aggressively on price.
rationale.unitEconomics: Thin staffing-industry margin, revenue-quality concerns from a few large accounts.
rationale.aiLeverage: AI-driven candidate matching and screening is a real efficiency lever here.
rationale.moat: Client contracts renew annually but are frequently rebid.
rationale.exitPath: Margin has to improve materially before this is more than a watch-list name.

deals.thesisNote: Interesting AI-leverage story, but underwriting doesn't clear on margin yet.

deals.name: Crosswinds Recruiting

rationale.fragmentation: Fragmented specialty-recruiting niche.
rationale.unitEconomics: Above-average margin for staffing given a specialty focus.
rationale.aiLeverage: Resume screening and pipeline scoring automation already partly implemented.
rationale.moat: Specialty expertise provides some differentiation, but relationships still walk out the door with recruiters.
rationale.exitPath: Reasonable bolt-on candidate; not platform-scale alone.

deals.thesisNote: IOI sent; likely bolt-on rather than a standalone platform.

deals.name: Pinnacle Workforce Group

rationale.fragmentation: Commoditized light-industrial staffing, dominated by a few large regional players.
rationale.unitEconomics: Sub-industry-average margin with high customer turnover.
rationale.aiLeverage: Some scheduling automation upside, insufficient to offset structural margin pressure.
rationale.moat: No differentiation versus larger competitors.
rationale.exitPath: No credible platform or bolt-on economics.

deals.thesisNote: Passed — commoditized segment, scale favors incumbents.

deals.name: Meridian Legal Support

rationale.fragmentation: Regional legal-support services remain highly fragmented outside the national e-discovery giants.
rationale.unitEconomics: 25% margin with multi-year law-firm contracts.
rationale.aiLeverage: Document review and first-pass drafting are directly automatable — among the strongest AI-leverage cases sourced.
rationale.moat: Law-firm switching costs are high once workflows are integrated.
rationale.exitPath: Strong platform candidate for a legal-support roll-up.

deals.thesisNote: LOI signed; the clearest AI-leverage-meets-moat combination in the pipeline.

deals.name: Sterling Paralegal Partners

rationale.fragmentation: Similar fragmentation profile to Meridian, smaller scale.
rationale.unitEconomics: Strong margin, slightly less diversified client base.
rationale.aiLeverage: Same document-automation upside as the vertical broadly.
rationale.moat: Decent retention, less entrenched than Meridian's workflows.
rationale.exitPath: Natural bolt-on under Meridian if the LOI closes.

deals.thesisNote: Queued as the first bolt-on candidate once Meridian closes.

deals.name: Barrister Support Services

rationale.fragmentation: Small, fragmented local market.
rationale.unitEconomics: Below-average margin for the vertical.
rationale.aiLeverage: Automation upside exists but is unproven at this scale.
rationale.moat: Short-term engagements, limited lock-in.
rationale.exitPath: Too small to be more than an opportunistic tuck-in.

deals.thesisNote: Low priority; screening only if valuation is unusually favorable.

deals.name: Claritas Medical Billing

rationale.fragmentation: RCM remains fragmented below the large national platforms.
rationale.unitEconomics: 23.5% margin with highly recurring, contracted revenue tied to claims volume.
rationale.aiLeverage: Claims-scrubbing and denial-management automation directly reduce headcount cost per claim.
rationale.moat: EHR integrations create real switching costs once embedded.
rationale.exitPath: Strongest platform candidate in the entire pipeline on a blended basis.

deals.thesisNote: Highest composite score sourced to date; diligence prioritized accordingly.

deals.name: Riverbend RCM Partners

rationale.fragmentation: Same fragmented regional RCM dynamic.
rationale.unitEconomics: Strong, slightly smaller-scale margin profile.
rationale.aiLeverage: Comparable automation upside to Claritas.
rationale.moat: Fewer EHR integrations than Claritas — somewhat less locked in.
rationale.exitPath: Credible bolt-on, or a second platform in a non-overlapping region.

deals.thesisNote: IOI sent; evaluating alongside Claritas for geographic overlap.

deals.name: Coastal Health Billing

rationale.fragmentation: Smaller regional player in a fragmented category.
rationale.unitEconomics: Below Claritas/Riverbend on margin.
rationale.aiLeverage: Same automation levers apply, less mature back office to build on.
rationale.moat: Limited EHR integration depth so far.
rationale.exitPath: Plausible bolt-on once a platform closes in-region.

deals.thesisNote: Early-stage; revisit once Claritas or Riverbend closes.

deals.name: Ironhide Freight Dispatch

rationale.fragmentation: Regional dispatch/brokerage market, moderately fragmented.
rationale.unitEconomics: Thin freight-brokerage margin, rate-cycle sensitive.
rationale.aiLeverage: Load-matching and routing automation is a genuine lever.
rationale.moat: Carrier and shipper relationships have limited lock-in.
rationale.exitPath: Cyclicality makes this a difficult standalone underwrite.

deals.thesisNote: AI story is real but margin cyclicality is the bigger risk factor.

deals.name: Lakeshore Last-Mile

rationale.fragmentation: Fragmented last-mile subcontractor market.
rationale.unitEconomics: Similar thin-margin profile to Ironhide.
rationale.aiLeverage: Route optimization directly cuts fuel and labor cost per stop.
rationale.moat: Contracts renew annually with a handful of retail clients.
rationale.exitPath: Only interesting as a bolt-on if a logistics platform materializes.

deals.thesisNote: Parked — not pursuing without a platform anchor first.

deals.name: Redline Logistics Co.

rationale.fragmentation: Small local player, limited consolidation impact alone.
rationale.unitEconomics: One customer is 74% of revenue at a 5% margin.
rationale.aiLeverage: Automation can't fix a single-customer concentration problem.
rationale.moat: No contract with the anchor customer — pure key-relationship risk.
rationale.exitPath: No viable path given concentration risk.

deals.thesisNote: Passed — concentration risk disqualifies regardless of AI story.

deals.name: Northstar Support Partners

rationale.fragmentation: Mid-market BPO space has room below the handful of national giants.
rationale.unitEconomics: Solid margin for the category, though seat-based pricing caps upside.
rationale.aiLeverage: AI-agent deflection of tier-1 contact volume is the entire investment thesis for this deal.
rationale.moat: Multi-year contracts with enterprise clients provide real stickiness.
rationale.exitPath: Platform-caliber if the AI-deflection roadmap executes as modeled.

deals.thesisNote: LOI signed; value creation plan is built entirely around the AI-deflection roadmap.

deals.name: Beacon BPO Group

rationale.fragmentation: Similar fragmented mid-market BPO dynamic to Northstar.
rationale.unitEconomics: Lower margin than Northstar, more seasonal client mix.
rationale.aiLeverage: Same deflection thesis applies, less mature contact-center tooling to build on.
rationale.moat: Shorter contract terms than Northstar.
rationale.exitPath: Reasonable bolt-on once Northstar's playbook is proven.

deals.thesisNote: Hold until Northstar's AI-deflection roadmap shows results.

deals.name: Clearline Contact Solutions

rationale.fragmentation: Smaller regional BPO, fragmented category.
rationale.unitEconomics: Below-average margin even for the category.
rationale.aiLeverage: Deflection upside exists but client contracts restrict tooling changes.
rationale.moat: Weak lock-in, frequent client rebids.
rationale.exitPath: Not platform-grade; marginal bolt-on at best.

deals.thesisNote: Low priority; contractual restrictions blunt the AI-leverage case.

deals.name: Willowbrook Property Group

rationale.fragmentation: Fragmented regional property-management market.
rationale.unitEconomics: Strong margin with recurring management-fee revenue.
rationale.aiLeverage: Tenant-communication and maintenance-dispatch automation help, but leasing still requires local presence.
rationale.moat: Multi-year management agreements with owners are hard to dislodge.
rationale.exitPath: Reasonable platform candidate, though less AI-differentiated than the top names in the pipeline.

deals.thesisNote: Solid fundamentals; AI story is more incremental than transformative here.

deals.name: Cornerstone Property Partners

rationale.fragmentation: Same fragmented category as Willowbrook, adjacent geography.
rationale.unitEconomics: Slightly thinner margin, smaller portfolio.
rationale.aiLeverage: Comparable automation profile to Willowbrook.
rationale.moat: Decent owner-contract retention.
rationale.exitPath: Natural bolt-on if Willowbrook or Highland closes first.

deals.thesisNote: Queued as a bolt-on candidate pending a platform close in the vertical.

deals.name: Highland Residential Management

rationale.fragmentation: Fragmented mountain-west property-management market.
rationale.unitEconomics: Strongest margin sourced in the vertical.
rationale.aiLeverage: AI-assisted leasing inquiries and maintenance triage implemented post-close.
rationale.moat: High owner-retention rate pre-close, improved further with integrated tooling.
rationale.exitPath: Second platform closed — anchor for future property-management bolt-ons.

deals.thesisNote: Closed. Reference case alongside BluePeak for underwriting future deals.
